deletionReadiness.rule: A legacy E2E entrypoint is deletion-ready only after equivalent Vitest scenario coverage exists, or after #4357 records an explicit retirement decision.
requires: same relevant CI lane or approved replacement lane
required secrets, skips, and runner requirements preserved
artifact and failure evidence at least as useful as the legacy script
migration status set to covered or retired

entries.notes: Partially exercised by the #5054 live-supported cloud OpenClaw Vitest scenario, but this broad script also covers install, gateway, sandbox, credentials, policy, and inference smoke behavior. Preserve until whole-script parity is recorded.
Partially exercised by #5054 cloud OpenClaw Vitest onboarding coverage, but delegated sandbox, policy, Landlock, and inference-local checks still need whole-script parity before deletion.
Provider routing and inference.local checks need typed inference/provider fixtures before deletion.
Provider switch behavior should migrate with the inference fixture family.
Split into Telegram, Discord, Slack, fake-provider, and token-rotation Vitest scenarios before deleting.
Hermetic WhatsApp pairing-QR size guard (NemoClaw#4522); migrate alongside the messaging-provider scenarios once a Vitest harness can drive the openclaw renderQrTerminal renderer.
Token rotation should migrate after provider fixtures can isolate per-provider state.
Credential redaction and sandbox-visible secret checks should migrate with the security fixture family.
Network policy checks need typed policy fixtures and preserved failure evidence.
Sandbox survival should migrate with lifecycle fixtures and stronger cleanup evidence.
Crash-loop recovery remains a late migration because it exercises failure timing and recovery classification.

Already uses Vitest, but still dispatches legacy remote shell suites; keep as a bridge until remote execution uses shared fixtures.

internalSurfaces.notes: Retire after the registry-driven Vitest runner owns phase ordering, expected-failure matching, cleanup, redaction, and artifact evidence.
Install, onboarding, lifecycle, probe, fixture, and context workers are bridge adapters until equivalent Vitest fixtures or CI setup actions own those phases.

Base install and preflight pass/fail assertion semantics are covered by the #5054 live registry Vitest onboarding fixture path. The legacy YAML/bash bridge still references these shell files, so deletionReady stays false until that bridge dependency is retired through #4357.
Runtime suite assertions migrate one family at a time into typed Vitest runtime helpers before this shell suite tree can be removed.
Runtime helper libraries stay only while bridge shell workers need shared environment, logging, context, teardown, or report rendering behavior.
